title: Historical Host-Resident Developer Workflow Hardening Planning
historical_note: This record preserves one earlier Dev Residency planning slice from before the canonical repo boundary moved to C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform on 2026-05-07. It is historical provenance, not a live host-workflow planning surface for current repo operations.
current_routing: use PROJECT_STATUS.md for the current residue-retirement lane and latest completed packets
use docs/architecture/OLARES-PUBLICATION-BOUNDARY-RETIREMENT-DEPENDENCY-INVENTORY-2026-05-06.md for the remaining post-cutover boundary closeout queue
use this record only when historical provenance is needed for the earlier Dev Residency 062 host-workflow planning slice preserved here
objective: Inventory the concrete remaining friction in the durable Olares host development posture and select exactly one smallest workflow-hardening slice for later execution.
dependency_note: Packet 061 selected host-resident workflow hardening as the next active Olares-first lane. The planning question is now which concrete remaining friction is smallest and highest truth to address next.

friction_inventory.description: The durable host workflow is split across the cutover technical plan, the general bootstrap runbook, the minimal MCP runbook, and separate tasks, but there is no single repo-owned host bootstrap/status entry surface.
evidence: `docs/architecture/OLARES-DEVELOPER-HOST-CUTOVER-TECHNICAL-PLAN-2026-05-05.md` defines the durable host operating model but not an operator entry command.
`docs/architecture/OLARES-AI-WORKFLOW-FIRST-SLICE-RUNBOOK-2026-05-06.md` defines minimal-MCP and hold-boundary commands separately.
`docs/OPERATOR-BOOTSTRAP-RUNBOOK.md` is workstation/bootstrap oriented and does not define a host-resident status surface.
`.vscode/tasks.json` currently exposes the hold-boundary cadence task but no unified host bootstrap/status task.

friction_inventory.description: Current durable-host checks are discoverable only by knowing which specific wrapper or runbook section applies.

The host-local toolchain proof exists in prior packets, but there is no single current-status command that reports whether the canonical host path still sees the admitted toolchains and validation helpers.

selected_slice_summary: The smallest truthful next hardening slice is to add one repo-owned host-resident bootstrap/status operator surface. That surface should report canonical mirror path/parity, host toolchain presence, minimal MCP readiness, and current hold-boundary status from `/home/olares/code/apex` without performing installs, runtime mutation, or broader workflow widening.
selected_later_execution_shape: Add one host-resident bootstrap/status script or wrapper under repo-owned operator surfaces.
Add one matching task entry for operators using the existing workspace/task contract.
Update the relevant Olares runbook(s) so the durable host workflow has one entry surface instead of scattered steps.
Validate that the new surface reports truthfully from the current host posture without widening beyond status/verification behavior.
rejected_as_next_slice: Generic documentation refresh with no operator surface change.
Reopening either dormant Olares branch without its listed trigger.
next_packet_candidate: Olares Dev Residency 063 - Host Bootstrap Status Operator Surface Execution
sql_permission_note: This planning packet authorizes no source/test mutation, no package or lockfile mutation, no installs, no runtime or service mutation, no AI-services expansion, no remote rewrite, no rollback or force/reset/clean, and no old-clone mutation.
